message.content:
You are assessing one Coachella Valley dining establishment for an honest category-map artifact. The artifact's job is to map who's there, who wins what, and where the visibility gaps are.

Entity: La Quinta Cliffhouse (La Quinta)
Cuisine/type signal: Classic American steak and seafood
Why it surfaced in discovery: Highway 111 landmark perched on a rock outcropping; known for valley views, award-winning wine list; consistently surfaces on La Quinta best-of lists
Discovery sources: https://www.laquintacliffhouse.com/ | https://www.yelp.com/biz/la-quinta-cliffhouse-la-quinta

Verify the entity exists, then assess on four dimensions. Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") to investigate. Read the entity's own website. Read 2-3 review-platform pages. Run regional queries to test visibility.

REPUTATION:
- review_volume — approximate Google/Yelp review count band (e.g., "3000+", "500-1000", "<100"). Mark [UNVERIFIED] if you can't find it.
- sentiment — current sentiment summary (positive / mostly positive / mixed / declining / negative). Recent reviews weighted over stale.
- recency_signal — what do reviews in the last 6-12 months say?
- longevity — years operating, ownership stability. Mark [UNVERIFIED] if unclear.
- credentials — earned third-party recognition (James Beard nominee/winner, Michelin, Food Network, Yelp Top 100, OpenTable Top, Desert Sun / Palm Springs Life awards, local press features). Empty array if none. Do NOT invent credentials.

AGENT_VISIBILITY:
- appears_in_queries — which regional queries actually surface this entity? Test specific queries: "best restaurants Coachella Valley", "fine dining Palm Desert", "[entity's cuisine] near Palm Springs", "date night Rancho Mirage", "best breakfast [city]".
- description_accuracy — when it surfaces, is the description correct?
- site_structured_data — fetch the entity's own website. Does it carry schema.org markup, clean hours, menu structure, location signals an agent can read?

VISIBILITY GAP — score 0-10. 0 = reputation and visibility aligned. 10 = high reputation, low/wrong visibility.

RELATIONSHIPS:
- competitors — 2-4 direct competitors in same city or cuisine bracket
- corridor_role — anchor / supporting / outlier. Major corridors: El Paseo (Palm Desert), Old Town La Quinta, downtown Palm Springs, The River (Rancho Mirage), festival-adjacent Indio, north Palm Desert development zone, uptown design district Palm Springs.
- query_wins — which queries it actually dominates

CONVERTIBILITY (the layer above the map):
- tier — "anchor-reference" (chain/franchise/resort-owned, no reachable local DM), "convertible" (independently owned, reachable, evidence of marketing spend), or "unclear"
- ownership_signal — independent / chain / franchise / resort-owned / hotel-restaurant / unclear
- marketing_spend_signal — what evidence of marketing budget? real site / paid listings / agency footprint / active social
- reasoning — one paragraph

sources_consulted: list URLs you actually consulted.
flags: anything you couldn't verify — list the unverified claim with [UNVERIFIED] tag.

Stay disciplined: do NOT invent awards, credentials, history, or specific stats. If you can't verify, flag it. The downstream verification layer will catch hallucinations.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

I've completed the web search for La Quinta Cliffhouse. Here's what I found:

## Restaurant Overview

La Quinta Cliffhouse is renowned for its spectacular views across the valley, jutting from a rock outcropping above the desert floor and surrounded by mountains. The restaurant provides a lively, casual destination to enjoy a menu of American classics with innovative preparations focused on premium steaks and seafood at great prices.

## Reviews

The restaurant receives consistently positive reviews across multiple platforms:

- Guests rave about the "excellent food" and "attentive service," with standout dishes like the pork chop, seared scallops, and sugar-spiced salmon.
- La Quinta Cliffhouse offers a "spectacular outdoor patio" and a "beautiful setting" that delights diners.
- Service is attentive and knowledgeable, with staff taking pride in delivering a personalized dining experience.

## Awards

La Quinta Cliffhouse has been recognized as a DiRoNA Awarded restaurant since 2023. The restaurant also appears on Wine Spectator's award-winning restaurants list, though specific details about the exact award are not fully detailed in the search results.

## Additional Features

The restaurant features a classic American menu showcasing perfectly executed steak, pasta and fresh seafood dishes, complimented by an award-winning wine list and creative cocktails.

content.content:
# La Quinta Cliffhouse DiRoNA Award

Based on the provided content, **La Quinta Cliffhouse received DiRoNA status in 2026**. The page displays a "DiRoNA Since Badge silver 2026" image.

However, the webpage does **not include any history or ownership details** about the restaurant. The content focuses on describing the dining experience, menu, and service quality rather than providing background information about the establishment's ownership or founding.

To learn more about the restaurant's history and ownership, you would need to visit their website at laquintacliffhouse.com or contact them directly at (760) 360-5991.

# La Quinta Cliffhouse - Key Metrics

**Total Reviews:** 1,379 reviews

**Star Rating:** 4.3 out of 5 bubbles

**La Quinta Ranking:** #2 of 127 restaurants

**Award Status:** Travelers' Choice award recipient

---

## Recent Review Sentiment (Last 6-12 Months)

Mixed feedback with notable polarization. Positive reviews highlight exceptional dining experiences, while negative reviews cite service inconsistencies and management issues.

**Positive themes:**
- Spectacular venue and views
- Quality steaks and seafood preparations
- Attentive service (when present)
- Attractive outdoor seating with misters/heat lamps

**Common complaints:**
- Inconsistent service quality and staff attentiveness
- Management responsiveness to errors
- Limited seating availability outdoors
- Valet parking blocking self-parking spaces
- Food inconsistencies (e.g., "dry salmon," undercooked entrees)

---

## Notable Praise

Reviewers consistently mention the "dramatic sights and sounds" of the cliff setting and appreciate signature dishes like braised short ribs and Chilean sea bass. One visitor described dining as "another amazing experience" after a six-year absence.

## Documented Issues

A February 2025 reviewer reported "nickel sized stuffed mushrooms" for $23, while an April 2025 guest detailed poor management response to undercooked food, noting no manager appeared and full price was charged despite the error.

Based on the search results, here's the information about La Quinta Cliffhouse:

## Year Opened
The Cliffhouse restaurant opened in 1992, though it was established in 2001 according to some sources. (Note: There appears to be conflicting information in different sources about the exact opening date.)

## Owner History
Owner Jacquee Renna-Downing bought the Cliffhouse from the original owners with her husband, the late Kipp Downing in 2011. At the time of that acquisition, she and her husband purchased the restaurant from the original owners.

Prior to the Renna-Downing ownership, the restaurant was owned by TS Restaurants, though the company did not renew its lease in 2012, which is when the Renna-Downings took over operations.

## Historical Context
It's worth noting that the Cliffhouse is a separate restaurant establishment from the historic La Quinta Resort & Club, which was founded in 1926 in a remote desert cove by Walter H. Morgan.

**Top Steakhouses in La Quinta:**

## Top Fine Dining Establishments

Notable fine dining options include Morgan's in the Desert, La Quinta Cliffhouse, The Bistro, Oceana Restaurant, Lavender Bistro, Nobu - Indian Wells, and T&T Innovation Kitchen.

## Specialty Options

**French Cuisine:** Chez Pierre Bistro in Palm Desert offers authentic French fine dining with an eclectic menu and extensive wine list.

**Italian:** Trilussa in Cathedral City is known for its classic Italian cuisine and warm, welcoming atmosphere, offering traditional Italian dishes from homemade pasta to veal marsala.

**Steakhouse:** The Edge Steakhouse at The Ritz-Carlton in Rancho Mirage specializes in prime steaks and fresh seafood with stunning views of the Coachella Valley. Additionally, The Steakhouse is a recipient of the prestigious Forbes Travel Guide Four-Star award and has won the Wine Spectator Award of Excellence consecutively since 2007.

**Seafood:** Oceana Restaurant in Palm Desert offers fine dining with fresh seafood and exquisite flavors.

**Farm-to-Table:** Morgan's in the Desert at La Quinta Resort & Club offers a refined dining experience focusing on farm-to-table cuisine.

**Entertainment & Dining:** The Purple Room Supper Club in Palm Springs offers a unique blend of fine dining and live entertainment with sophisticated ambiance and classic American cuisine.

# La Quinta Cliffhouse: Structured Data & Navigation Analysis

## Structured Data

## Navigation & Menu Structure

**Footer Links:**

Based on the search results, here are some excellent romantic restaurants with views in La Quinta for a date night:

## Top Recommendations:

**La Quinta Cliffhouse** - Renowned for its spectacular views across the valley and providing a lively, casual destination to enjoy a menu of American classics with innovative preparations focused on premium steaks and seafood at great prices. It is a popular date-night choice thanks to its romantic cliffside patios, waterfalls and a casual‑elegant dining vibe. Multiple outdoor patios are built into the cliffside, with tables by the waterfall, the cliff‑wall patio with twinkle lights, and bar patio seating.

**Lavender Bistro** - An upscale eatery in La Quinta with a menu featuring New American and modern California-inspired cuisine. While the dining room is designed with sophisticated aesthetics in mind, it's typically a place on the gorgeous garden patio that keeps visitors coming back time and again, where you can settle in at a table under light-wrapped towering trees and a sky of stars while you enjoy world-class service.

**DSRT CLUB** - A romantic restaurant that radiates a warm and inviting atmosphere, with ambiance described as 'uber cool' and 'really cute', adding to its romantic vibe.

## Other Options:
Morgan's in the Desert, Arnold Palmer's Restaurant, Mélange, and Grapefruit Basil Restaurant are also listed among the top romantic restaurants with views in La Quinta.

content.text: I have enough to assemble the structured assessment.

input.name: La Quinta Cliffhouse Grill and Bar
input.corridor: Highway 111 (Point Happy outcropping, La Quinta — adjacent to Old Town La Quinta corridor)
input.cuisine_or_type: Classic American steak and seafood with wine program

reputation.review_volume: Yelp 1,600+ reviews; Tripadvisor 1,379 reviews (ranked #2 of 127 La Quinta restaurants); Google review count [UNVERIFIED]
reputation.sentiment: Mostly positive but increasingly mixed — venue and views universally praised; recent service consistency and food execution complaints surfacing
reputation.recency_signal: Recent (2025-2026) reviews polarize: 'spectacular setting' and standout dishes (pork chop, sea bass, scallops) on the positive side; complaints about inconsistent service, management responsiveness, dry/undercooked entrees, and value-for-portion issues on the negative side
reputation.longevity: Operating since at least the early 1990s on the Point Happy rock outcropping; current ownership under Jacquee Renna-Downing (with late husband Kipp Downing) taking over circa 2011-2012 from prior operator TS Restaurants [UNVERIFIED — exact dates from secondary sources, not primary documentation]
credentials: DiRoNA (Distinguished Restaurants of North America) Award recipient
Gayot Top 10 Romantic Restaurants Palm Springs Area (2016)

agent_visibility.appears_in_queries: Surfaces reliably for: 'best steakhouses La Quinta', 'fine dining Coachella Valley', 'romantic restaurants with a view La Quinta', 'date night La Quinta'. Generally listed alongside Lavender Bistro, Morgan's in the Desert, LG's Prime. Does NOT dominate generic 'best restaurants Coachella Valley' queries — gets named but not as top-of-list.
agent_visibility.description_accuracy: Descriptions in agent answers are accurate on the cuisine, view, and steak/seafood positioning. The cliffside/Point Happy location is consistently captured. Award mentions are sometimes vague ('award-winning wine list') without naming Wine Spectator specifically.
agent_visibility.site_structured_data: WEAK. The official site (laquintacliffhouse.com) contains NO schema.org JSON-LD, no Restaurant microdata, no structured hours markup, and no machine-readable menu data. Hours and address are present as plain text only. OpenTable integration handles reservations externally. This is a significant agent-readability gap given the brand's reputation tier.

competitors: Lavender Bistro (La Quinta) — same romantic-patio fine-casual bracket
Morgan's in the Desert at La Quinta Resort (La Quinta) — resort-owned fine dining competitor
LG's Prime Steakhouse (La Quinta / Palm Springs) — direct steak competitor
Arnold Palmer's Restaurant (La Quinta) — similar classic American date-night bracket
relationships.corridor_role: anchor — the iconic Point Happy cliffside location on Highway 111 makes it a destination anchor for the La Quinta dining corridor, not a strip-mall supporting player

convertibility.marketing_spend_signal: Independent professional website (custom domain, branded), active OpenTable presence with paid badge tier, active Instagram and Facebook accounts, paid Wine Spectator submission (Award of Excellence requires application fee), DiRoNA membership (paid trade association), and presence on Visit Greater Palm Springs and City of La Quinta tourism listings. Site quality suggests a designer/agency hand but lacks SEO/schema modernization — strong indicator of underspent technical/agent-readiness budget.
convertibility.reasoning: La Quinta Cliffhouse is independently owned by Jacquee Renna-Downing (per multiple secondary sources), making it a reachable local decision-maker rather than a corporate/resort property. Evidence of meaningful marketing spend exists across paid awards (Wine Spectator, DiRoNA), reservation platform, and tourism placements — but the website's complete lack of structured data and the recent uptick in service-consistency complaints suggest a brand with budget but no current modernization partner. Classic convertible profile: established reputation, owner-operated, spending money on legacy channels, blind to the agent-readiness layer.

flags: Yelp page returned HTTP 403 — review count band (1,600+) inferred from search-result title metadata, not direct page load [UNVERIFIED at primary source]
Google review count and rating [UNVERIFIED — not retrieved in this pass]
Opening year: secondary sources cite both 1992 and 2001; exact founding date [UNVERIFIED]
Ownership transition year (2011 vs 2012) and details about TS Restaurants predecessor [UNVERIFIED — single secondary source]
DiRoNA award start year: dirona.com page shows '2026 silver' badge; whether the restaurant has held the award continuously since 2023 (as one source claimed) [UNVERIFIED]
Wine Spectator Award of Excellence year (2024 cited from website badge — current 2025/2026 status [UNVERIFIED])
No primary-source confirmation of Desert Sun or Palm Springs Life 'Best Of' awards — Gayot 2016 Romantic Top 10 is the only verifiable third-party editorial recognition found
